q: In Young's double slit experiment, alternate bright and dark lines seen on the screen are explained as interference phenomenon.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, a circular wavelet emerges from it according to Huygens' principle.
In the double slit experiment, each slit acts as a secondary source of circular waves.
Two waves are coherent if they are of same frequency, same amplitude, and are in phase.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called fringes.
Constructive interference occurs at the centre of the screen because waves travel the same distance and are in phase.
A crest of one wave arriving at the same time as a trough of the other wave results in constructive interference.
If the path difference is equal to an integral number of wavelengths $(0, \lambda, 2\lambda, \dots)$, the waves will interfere constructively.
Constructive interference results in dark fringes on the screen.
If the path difference is equal to a half-integral number of wavelengths $(\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, \dots)$, the waves will interfere destructively.
The position of fringes on the screen is given by the distance from the central bright fringe, denoted as $y$.
For a bright fringe to be formed, path difference must be equal to an integral number of wavelengths: $d \sin \theta = m \lambda$.
The zeroth order ($m = 0$) bright corresponds to the central bright line at $\theta = 0$.
The first-order brights ($m = 1$) can be seen on only one side of the central line.
The distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the fringe width.
The position of fringes and fringe width are independent of the wavelength of the light source.
The fringe width $\Delta y$ is given by the formula $\Delta y = \frac{\lambda D}{d}$.
Bright and dark fringes are formed alternatively where bright fringe has maximum intensity and intensity of dark fringe is zero.
In Young's experiment, two waves from each source will ___ and as a result, an interference pattern will be displayed on the screen.
The central bright fringe is formed because waves reaching the centre of the screen travel the ___ distance.
What is the condition for two waves to be coherent?
a: Same frequency, same amplitude and in phase

q: In the formula $y = m \frac{\lambda D}{d}$, what does $m$ represent?
What type of interference leads to a dark fringe?
What happens at the centre of the screen in a double-slit experiment?
The distance between the $m^{th}$ and $(m+1)^{th}$ order bright fringe is known as the ___.
